figures.caption: Table 1 Nominal-design comparison of the baseline and sparse feedback leader controllers at (T, |L|, τmax) = (8.0 s, 8, 0.25 s) over M = 60 independent realizations. Tolerances are given in (9.1). Intervals are 95% Wilson score confidence intervals. Bold marks the more favorable entry where the comparison is clearly favorable.

Fig. 1 Computational results for the finite-N system. (a) Controller comparison at the nominal design point (T, |L|, τmax) = (8.0 s, 8, 0.25 s), M = 60: bars show P̂ (tube) with 95% Wilson score confidence intervals, and the inset reports the mean L1 actuation cost and leader duty cycle. (b) Sparse-controller success probability across the terminal-time sweep T ∈ {5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10} s, M = 30, showing the feasible window at T ∈ [7, 8] s. (c) Sensitivity to leader count |L| ∈ {4, 6, 8, 10, 12}, M = 30, showing a non-monotone dependence with best tested performance at |L| = 8. (d) Sensitivity to the delay bound τmax ∈ {0.00, 0.10, 0.25, 0.40, 0.55} s, M = 30. All error bars are 95% Wilson score intervals, and the dashed line marks the required reliability level 1− α = 0.95.
Table 2 Principal notation used throughout the paper, with the regularization parameters and the tube tolerances now typographically distinct.
